analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet notes COVID-19 caused major disruption to education/training in Europe and invites the public to participate in an EU consultation to build inclusive, high-quality digital education (#EUHaveYourSay). Evaluation by narrative: EU1 (successful story): Not showcased; no retrospective celebration of EU success or learning from past beyond a factual disruption mention. EU2 (united): No explicit emphasis on EU unity or solidarity among member states. EU3 (useful for its citizens): Yes. It promotes an EU public consultation and positions the EU as proactively addressing citizens’ needs (improving digital education inclusively and with quality), implying practical usefulness within the EU. EU4 (avant-garde actor): Partly implied through focus on digital education, but the tweet does not explicitly claim leadership or pioneering regulation; cautious no. EU5 (global champion of fair causes): Not addressed. EU6 (contributes to a better world): Not about global actions; focus is internal. Conclusion: The tweet promotes EU3.
